target: 3. Bonds of Close Friends
You kept me waiting.

Ah, Hizuru-chan!
Were you at the toilet too?
I left my things in the room and came out right away.

Is that so?
I felt like I waited a long time though.
…Maybe time perception differs with age.
Huh? What?

No, it's nothing.
So, where are you going to show me?
Hmm, where should we go?
You said all that, but you don't even have a plan…
Let's go see the Hero of Justice then.
Huh?

(Is it about a fictional hero?
No… such things probably wouldn't be exciting)

(Well, it doesn't matter right now.
If Mio's happy and gets it over with quickly, that's fine)

Ah, that's fine.
Take me to this so-called Hero of Justice.

Got it!
Come on, follow me!
She's unnecessarily energetic…

(This island's changed a bit, Ryunosuke said…
But…)
(It's basically the same as when I lived here)
(Always welcoming me with the same face - my hometown…)

(Well, it's a bit cliché, but not bad.
Having a place to return to is good)
Huhuhu~♪

(These kinds of
nonsense personified
can also attack)
Hey, Hizuru-chan!
What?

Thinking about stories,
that's your job, right?
What kind of story are you writing?

If you say "what kind," I can't answer.
Your question's too vague.

I saw it before when Dad showed it to me,
but the characters were too hard to read.
They're small too.

Not that kind,
can't you write something like a picture book?
Then I could read it too!

(A picture book…
Maybe diving into an entirely new genre
would help break the slump…)

(No, I can't do picture books.
I'm already being influenced by Mio's thoughts)

Ah, it's Mio and Tokiko!
Hey~!
Hello, Mio-chan.

Hello.
You're always so energetic.
And that person…

This is Hizuru-chan!
She's Dad's older sister!

Ryunosuke's older sister…
I'm Hizuru Minakata.

Ah, the older sister of Ryunosuke-san!
I'm Mio Kofune.
I'm Tokiko Hishigata.
(The Kofune and Hishigata families…)

Ah, the Western-style restaurant and Hishigata Clinic.
I was often helped by both when I lived here.

(Compared to before, the impression has changed a lot.
Indeed, at this age, it's not uncommon to become a completely different person after a few years)

Mio and Tokiko are really close.
They always do things together.

Always doing things together…
I don't think we're that close.

Haha, but we met yesterday too.
We're supposed to study together tomorrow, right?

S-since it's the summer vacation assignments…
I need to get them done…
But I'm alone…
Sure, I'll help you with the parts you don't understand.

Thanks!
I'll return the favor someday.

It's fine.
I enjoy being with Mio.

…My own time,
  can be freely given for others.
  Of course, without charge.
Huh?

And this isn't one-sided service either.
Is it?

O-of course it is!
If Tokiko helps me,
I'll help Tokiko too!
Mio…

Hmm…
That's what friends are.

F-friends…?
It feels a bit embarrassing.
But it's not wrong, right?
Yeah, it's not wrong.

(Saying it out loud might sound cheap,
but…)

(But if you dig deeper, it's a theme
that would take a lifetime to write about)

(But even between friends,
there are plenty of cases where the relationship breaks)

(A story about being betrayed by a friend.
Not a useless theme at all)
But for what reason would they betray?

(Love?
No, I don't want to write a melodrama)
Um… Hizuru-san?

Let me ask directly.
If one of you betrayed the other, what would you do?
Huh!?
Betrayal…?
Ah, I want to hear your thoughts.

S-suddenly asking something like that…
Tokiko would never do that,
and of course I wouldn't…

Imagine it!
What's the point of having thinking ability!
Uhh…

…At first, I'd be angry.
But I'd also feel sad…
I'd think a lot about why…
In the end, I'd probably forgive them.

That's right…
I'd do the same as Tokiko.
Even if it was a cruel betrayal?

Yeah…
If Tokiko betrayed me, there must be a good reason.

I'd talk to her directly and resolve it together.
I don't want to fight and break up with Tokiko.

Also, if…
I was the one who betrayed them…

Unless it was something really serious,
I'd talk to them before betraying.
Yeah, that's right!

(Being angry but forgiving…
Talking before betraying…)
(Ordinary! There's no fun at all!!)

(There are situations in the world that are hard to talk about.
No matter how strong the bond, there are things you can't resist)

(But… their smiles toward each other seem to say
"Such things don't exist"…)

(This is what 'friends' mean.
With a strong bond, you can overcome anything)

(Thinking back, I haven't really paid attention to this concept before.
In that sense, it's refreshing…)

Thank you.
It was helpful.

Helpful…?
What's it helpful for…?
Ah, don't mind it.
H-huh…?

(Young… 
It's really young and straightforward answers)

(There are things in the world that people can't talk about.
No matter how strong the bond, there are things you can't resist)

(But if there's a strong trust that can overcome even that…
)
I see… Not a bad theme at all.
(The problem is that I'm pretty unfamiliar with this 'friend' relationship)

I wonder what's wrong?
She's been staring into the sky, thinking for so long…
She's a bit strange too.
